---
title: "Report on the use of passive acoustic monitoring for songbirds and seabirds on Lanz and Cox Islands"
format:
  html:
    grid:
      margin-width: 300px
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    papersize: letter
navbar: right
theme: cosmo
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
author: 
  - name: "Alex MacPhail"
    affiliation: "Biodiversity Pathways Ltd."
  - name: "Kevin Kelly"
    affiliation: "Biodiversity Pathways Ltd."
  - name: "Victoria Berg"
    affiliation: "Biodiversity Pathways Ltd."
  - name: "Erica McClaren"
    affiliation: "BC Ministry of Environment and Parks"
  - name: "Iain Reid"
    affiliation: "BC Ministry of Environment and Parks"
editor: visual
bibliography: references.bib
nocite: '@*'
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
toc-expand: true
prefer-html: true
toc-location: left
styles: styles.css
github: https://github.com/biodiversitypathways/lanz-and-cox

---

![](assets/lanz-and-cox-banner.png){style="float:left`;" fig-alt="Lanz and Cox Islands (c) Iain Reid" fig-align="center"}

```{r}
#| label: Load packages and authenticate to WildTrax
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(wildrtrax)
library(tidyverse)
library(leaflet)
library(vegan)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggridges)

wildrtrax::wt_auth(force = TRUE)

load("lanz-and-cox.RData")
#save.image("lanz-and-cox.RData")

```

```{r}
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

bcsi <- wt_download_report(4021, 'ARU', c('main','ai','recording'))

bcsi_main <- bcsi[[2]] |>
  mutate(year = year(recording_date_time))

lac_locs <- wt_get_sync("organization_locations", organization = 5853)

```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" style="background-color: #f4f4f4; padding: 20px;"}
This report is dynamically generated, meaning its results may evolve with the addition of new data or further analyses. For the most recent updates, refer to the publication date and feel free to reach out to the authors.
:::

# Abstract

Passive acoustic monitoring was conducted on Lanz and Cox Islands to characterize seabird and songbird communities. Autonomous recording units (ARUs) were deployed at `r length(unique(bcsi_locs$location))` locations and analyzed using a combination of machine-learning algorithms and expert validation. This report summarizes species presence, temporal patterns of detection, and relative abundance for seabirds and songbirds, and provides spatial representations of detections across both islands. These results establish a baseline to support evaluation of seabird prospecting activity and future changes in avian communities.

# Land Acknowledgement

The land and sea in the Scott Islands are located within the territories of the Quatsino and Tlatlasikwala First Nations. BC Parks and Environment and Climate Change Canada – Canadian Wildlife Service are working in partnership with Quatsino and Tlatlasikwala First Nations and for this part of the larger project, they would like to express their gratitude to T̕łat̕łasikwa̱la First Nation member Anne Wilson and Quatsino First Nation member Graem Hall for providing in-field support during deployment and collection of audio recording units on Lanz and Cox Islands.

# Funding Acknowledgement

This project was funded in part by the BC License Plate program.

![](assets/license-plate.jpg){style="float:left`;" fig-alt="Government of British Columbia (c)" fig-align="center"}

# Introduction

Lanz and Cox Islands support important seabird and songbird habitats, yet populations have been negatively affected by the presence of invasive predators. As part of a broader restoration initiative, passive acoustic monitoring was implemented to document avian community composition. Autonomous recording units (ARUs) were deployed across both islands over three breeding seasons to capture vocal activity of seabirds and songbirds. Acoustic data were processed using the WildTrax (@macphail2026wildtrax) data management platform in combination with BirdNET (@kahl2021birdnet) and HawkEars (@huus2025hawkears) machine-learning algorithms, with detections verified using expert review informed by prior experience in marine acoustic environments. This report presents the results of these analyses, including species detected, timing of detections, relative abundance metrics, and spatial patterns of occurrence. Together, these data provide a baseline for monitoring that can be compared to assess other project outcomes and ecological recovery.

# Methods

## Data collection

ARUs were deployed in three stages between 2024 and 2025 across Lanz and Cox Islands (@fig-aru-monitoring-locations), with the final data retrievals taking place in the winter of 2025-26. Sites were accessed via helicopter. Recordings were collected on a schedule of recording every five minutes every 15 minutes at 24000 Hz. A total of `r nrow(bcsi_recs)` recordings were collected totalling `r bcsi_recs |> summarise(sum = round(sum(recording_duration) / 3600)) |> pull(sum) |> prettyNum(big.mark = ",")` audio hours of data (@fig-bcsi-recs).

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Locations from Lanz and Cox Provincial Park ARU Monitoring Program
#| label: fig-aru-monitoring-locations

m <- leaflet() %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addCircleMarkers(
    data = lac_locs,
    popup = ~paste("Location:", location, "<br>"),
    fillOpacity = 1,
    color = "black", 
    radius = 6 
  ) %>%
  addMeasure(primaryLengthUnit = "meters", primaryAreaUnit = "sqmeters") %>%
  addMiniMap(position = "bottomleft")

if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  m
} else {
  map_file <- "map_aru_locations.png"
  if (!file.exists(map_file)) mapview::mapshot2(m, file = map_file)
  knitr::include_graphics(map_file)
}

```

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| label: tbl-loc-summary
#| tbl-cap: Locations surveyed across years. Ones indicated a deployment in that year for that location

bcsi_locs <- bcsi_main |>
  mutate(year = year(recording_date_time)) |>
  select(location, year, latitude, longitude) |>
  distinct() |>
  drop_na(latitude) |>
  mutate(surveyed = 1) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = surveyed, values_fill = 0)

kable(bcsi_locs)

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: false

seabirds <- c(
  "Tufted Puffin",
  "Common Murre",
  "Cassin's Auklet",
  "Rhinoceros Auklet",
  "Short-tailed Albatross",
  "Black-footed Albatross",
  "Pink-footed Shearwater",
  "Marbled Murrelet",
  "Ancient Murrelet",
  "Black Oystercatcher",
  "Leach's Storm-petrel"
)

# wt_get_species() |>
#   filter(species_common_name %in% seabirds)
# 
# wt_get_project_species(4069) |>
#   filter(species_common_name %in% seabirds)

bcsi_recs <- wt_get_sync("organization_recordings", organization = 5853)

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Recordings collected from Lanz and Cox Provincial Park ARU Monitoring Program
#| label: fig-bcsi-recs

bcsi_recs |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(recording_date_time)) |>
  count(date) |>
  ggplot(aes(x=date, y=n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Number of recordings",
    title = "BCSI recording collection over time"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Data management, processing and quality control

All recordings were uploaded to [WildTrax](https://wildtrax.ca) in order for the data to be accessible and stored. Locations name were confirmed against the deployment information to ensure data from each location was consistently named across years.

## Community data processing

For each sampling location and year, a total of nine autonomous recording unit (ARU) recordings, each 3 minutes in duration, were analyzed to characterize the songbird community. All vocalizing species were identified, and the abundance of each species was estimated using a count-removal framework based on time-to-first-detection, which helps reduce positive bias associated with repeated detections of the same individual within a recording. Recordings were intentionally distributed across diel periods to capture variation in vocal activity. Five recordings were selected during the dawn period (04:00–07:59), when songbird detectability is typically highest. Two recordings were selected during dusk (19:00–22:59), and two during the night (23:00–03:59), allowing for detection of crepuscular or nocturnally active species and providing a more complete representation of the avian community. This standardized temporal sampling design ensured consistent effort across locations and years while maximizing detectability across species with differing activity patterns. Coastal proximity may reduce ARU detection effectiveness, as wave action and wind-driven noise can mask vocalizations and shrink effective detection distances (@pijankowski2011landscape). We therefore examined whether distance to the nearest coastline predicted observed species richness across monitoring locations. We also compared geophonic acoustic indices (@towsey2017calculation) against WildTrax noise metrics to establish thresholds for task exclusion and to characterize background noise conditions at each site.

## Use of automated classifiers on seabirds

We first wanted to assess the accuracy of the current version of the HawkEars classifier for three focal species in the group: Common Murre, Marbled Murrelet, and Black Oystercatcher. We extracted all recordings from WildTrax in which the classifier detected these species with a mean score of \~0.33 used. A human reviewer then verified each flagged task, confirming detections as true positives (TP) where the species was audible, or false positives (FP) where no supporting vocalization could be identified. Using these verified results, we applied the `wt_evaluate_classifier()` and `wt_classifier_threshold()` functions from the wildrtrax R package to calculate precision, recall, and F-score across a range of score thresholds. This allowed us to identify the minimum confidence threshold at which classifier performance was deemed acceptable for each species, balancing the trade-off between retaining true detections and excluding false positives. Recordings scoring below this optimized threshold were excluded from subsequent analyses, while those meeting or exceeding it were accepted without further manual review. This approach is consistent with emerging best practices for deploying automated classifiers at scale, where full human review is impractical and a validated threshold provides a reproducible and defensible basis for data inclusion.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true

bcsi_seabirds <- wt_download_report(c(4069,4021), 'ARU', c('main','ai'))

bcsi_seabirds[[1]] |> 
  filter(species_common_name %in% seabirds) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = confidence)) + 
  geom_density() + 
  theme_bw() + 
  ylab("Density") + 
  xlab("HawkEars Score")

eval <- wt_evaluate_classifier(bcsi_seabirds, resolution = "recording", remove_species = TRUE)
# wt_additional_species(bcsi_seabirds, remove_species = TRUE, threshold = 0.8, resolution = "task")
```

Next we continued to intensify our evaluation of classifiers by using HawkEars 2.0 here.

# Results

## Songbird species richness and diversity

A total of `r nrow(distinct_spp)` species were found with a summary of detections found at @tbl-spp-summary. @fig-spp-rich-locs describes the relationship of species richness for each location across the two years of surveys. Shannon's diversity was also calculated (see @fig-shannon).

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| label: tbl-spp-summary
#| collapse: true
#| code-fold: true
#| tbl-cap: Count of detections per species

kable(bcsi_main |>
        wt_tidy_species(remove = c("unknown")) |>
  group_by(species_common_name) |>
  filter(!species_common_name == "NONE") |>
  tally() |>
  arrange(-n) |>
  rename(Species = species_common_name, `Count of detections` = n))

```

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false

spp_rich_location <- bcsi_main |>
  as_tibble() |>
  wt_tidy_species(remove = c("amphibian","abiotic","insect","unknown"), zerofill = F) |>
  mutate(year = lubridate::year(recording_date_time)) |>
  select(location, year, species_code) |>
  distinct() |>
  group_by(location, year) |>
  summarise(species_count = n_distinct(species_code)) |>
  ungroup()

distinct_spp <- bcsi_main |>
  as_tibble() |>
  wt_tidy_species(remove = c("amphibian","abiotic","insect","unknown"), zerofill = F) |>
  mutate(year = lubridate::year(recording_date_time)) |>
  select(species_code) |>
  distinct() |>
  arrange(species_code)
```

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Species richness at forest monitoring locations across years
#| label: fig-spp-rich-locs

spp_rich_location |>
  ggplot(aes(x=as.factor(year), y=species_count, fill=as.factor(year), group=as.factor(year))) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  xlab('Year') + ylab('Species richness') +
  ggtitle('Species richness at each location surveyed for each year') +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| results: hide
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Shannon diversity index over years
#| label: fig-shannon
#| cap-location: bottom
#| code-fold: true

bcsi_main |> 
  wt_tidy_species(remove = c("amphibian","abiotic","insect","unknown"), zerofill = F) |>
  inner_join(wt_get_species() |> select(species_code, species_class, species_order), by = "species_code") |>
  select(location, recording_date_time, species_code, species_common_name, individual_order, abundance) |>
  distinct() |>
  group_by(location, recording_date_time, species_code, species_common_name) |>
  summarise(count = max(individual_order)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = species_code, values_from = count, values_fill = 0) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = -(location:species_common_name), names_to = "species", values_to = "count") |>
  group_by(location, year = year(recording_date_time), species) |>
  summarise(total_count = sum(count)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  group_by(location, year) |>
  summarise(shannon_index = diversity(total_count, index = "shannon")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year), y = shannon_index, fill = factor(year))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, colour = "grey") +
  labs(x = "Year",
       y = "Shannon diversity index per location") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(alpha = 0.7, option = "turbo") +
  ylim(0,3)

```

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| results: hide
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Seasonal detection activity of most commonly detected (n > 3) forest species
#| label: fig-spp-activity

bcsi_main |>
  wt_tidy_species(remove = c("amphibian","abiotic","insect","unknown"), zerofill = T) |>
  filter(!species_code == "NONE") |>
  select(location, recording_date_time, species_common_name, species_code, abundance) |>
  mutate(julian = lubridate::yday(recording_date_time),
         month = month(recording_date_time),
         year = factor(year(recording_date_time))) |>
  arrange(species_code) |>
  group_by(species_code) |>
  add_tally() |>
  ungroup() |>
  filter(!n<3) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = month, y = species_common_name)) + 
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.4, scale = 4) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:12, labels = month.abb) +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Month") + 
  ylab("Species")

```

### Proximity to ocean and noise effects

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Individuals detected versus environmental noise
#| label: fig-ind-noise

noise_level_map <- c(
  "Low"     = 1,
  "Medium"  = 2,
  "High"    = 3,
  "Extreme" = 4
)

noise_pattern_map <- c(
  "Intermittent" = 2,
  "Infrequently"   = 1,
  "Frequent"     = 3,
  "Constant"     = 4
)

bcsi_main |>
  separate_rows(max_noise_volume, sep = ",") |>
  mutate(
    max_noise_volume = str_trim(max_noise_volume),
    max_noise_volume = factor(max_noise_volume, 
                              levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High", "Extreme"))
  ) |>
  group_by(location, recording_date_time, max_noise_volume) |>
  summarise(n = sum(!is.na(individual_order)), .groups = "drop") |>
  drop_na() |>
  ggplot(aes(max_noise_volume, n, fill = max_noise_volume)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    x = "Noise level",
    y = "Individuals detected per recording"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo")

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Noise per location on Scott Islands
#| label: fig-noise-loc

bcsi_main |>
  separate_rows(max_noise_volume, sep = ",") |>
  mutate(max_noise_volume = str_trim(max_noise_volume),
         noise_score = case_when(max_noise_volume == "Low" ~ 1, max_noise_volume == "Medium" ~ 2, max_noise_volume == "High" ~ 3, max_noise_volume == "Extreme" ~ 4)) |>
  drop_na(noise_score) |>
  select(location, recording_date_time, noise_score) |>
  distinct() |>
  group_by(location) |>
  summarise(median_noise = median(noise_score),
            q25 = quantile(noise_score, 0.25),
            q75 = quantile(noise_score, 0.75)) |>
  mutate(location = fct_reorder(location, median_noise)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = median_noise, y = location)) +
  geom_linerange(aes(xmin = q25, xmax = q75), linewidth = 1, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_point(aes(colour = median_noise), size = 4) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:4, labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High", "Extreme")) +
  scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "turbo", guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = "Noise level",
    y = "Location"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Classifier results

### Evaluation of HawkEars v1.0.8

Precision-recall curves were generated to evaluate classifier performance across all confidence thresholds for both Birdnet v2.1 and HawkEars v1.0.8. At low recall values (≤ 0.25), both classifiers achieved comparable precision (\~0.65), indicating that high-confidence detections from either classifier are similarly reliable. As recall increased, classifier performance diverged: Birdnet v2.1 maintained a moderately higher precision across mid-to-high recall values (0.25–1.0), stabilizing around 0.30 at full recall. HawkEars declined more steeply, reaching \~0.13 at high recall, though it exhibited considerable variability across species (wide confidence interval) particularly between recall values of 0.25–0.75. These results suggest that Birdnet v2.1 is more conservative and consistent when detections are aggregated across species, while HawkEars shows greater species-level variability in the precision-recall tradeoff. The optimal operating threshold for both classifiers lies at low-to-moderate recall, where precision remains above 0.50.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: HawkEars 1.0.8 performance evaluation
#| label: fig-he-perform

wt_classifier_threshold(eval)

ggplot(eval, aes(x=recall, y=precision, colour=classifier)) +
  geom_smooth() +
  theme_bw() +
  xlab("Classifier Recall") +
  ylab("Classifier Precision") +
  ylim(0,1)

```

### HawkEars 2.0

Initial runs of HawkEars 2.0 were conducted yielding a total of `r nrow(bcsi_he2)` detections were generated from the classification process with raw data visible in @fig-raw-he2. Across all locations and classifier confidence score bins, MAMU consistently dominated the proportion of detections, particularly at higher confidence thresholds (0.75–1), where it accounted for the vast majority of hits at nearly all DC and DL sites. CAAU and COMU were the next most frequently detected species, contributing moderate proportions across the low-to-mid confidence bins (0–0.25 and 0.25–0.5), with their presence declining relative to MAMU as classifier confidence increased. ANMU appeared most prominently in the lower confidence bins at select DC locations, suggesting either lower detection certainty or lower site-level abundance. RHAU and TUPU were detected at notably lower proportions overall, with TUPU appearing sporadically at a handful of locations, most visibly at DC10 and DL12, predominantly in the lower confidence range. As classifier confidence increased toward the 0.75–1 bin, the community composition simplified considerably, with MAMU detections becoming overwhelmingly dominant and the contribution of other species diminishing across nearly all locations. This pattern suggests that higher-confidence classifier scores are selectively capturing MAMU calls with greater reliability, while lower-confidence bins reflect a broader and more uncertain mix of seabird vocalizations across the surveyed sites. 

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true

bcsi_he2 <- wt_get_sync("project_aru_tags", project = 4346)

make_pie <- function(loc, data) {
  data |>
    filter(location == loc) |>
    count(species_code) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = "", y = n, fill = species_code)) +
    geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    labs(
      title = paste(loc, "Species Detections"),
      fill  = "Species"
    ) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d()
}

ggplot_to_html <- function(p) {
  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp, plot = p, width = 4, height = 4, dpi = 100)
  encoded <- base64enc::base64encode(tmp)
  as.character(tags$img(src = paste0("data:image/png;base64,", encoded),
                        width = "300px"))
}

lac_locs2 <- lac_locs |>
  mutate(
    pie_chart = map(location, make_pie, data = bcsi_he2),
    pie_chart_html = map_chr(pie_chart, ggplot_to_html)
  )

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-cap: Raw HawkEars 2.0 detections
#| label: fig-raw-he2

m2 <- leaflet(lac_locs2) %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addMarkers(
    icon = ~pie_chart,
    popup = ~pie_chart_html
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    colors = pal,
    labels = names(pal),
    title = "Species"
  ) %>%
  addMiniMap(position = "bottomleft") %>%
  addMeasure(primaryLengthUnit = "meters", primaryAreaUnit = "sqmeters")

if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  m2
} else {
  map_file <- "map_hawkears2.png"
  if (!file.exists(map_file)) mapview::mapshot2(m2, file = map_file)
  knitr::include_graphics(map_file)
}

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-cap: Raw HawkEars 2.0 detections; describes proportion of hits across each locations across classifier scores bins
#| label: fig-raw-he2-proportions

bcsi_he2 %>%
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(species_individual_comments),
    score = ifelse(score < 0 | score > 1, NA, score),
    score_bin = cut(
      score,
      breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = c("0–0.25", "0.25–0.5", "0.5–0.75", "0.75–1")
    ),
    score_bin = factor(
      score_bin,
      levels = c("0–0.25", "0.25–0.5", "0.5–0.75", "0.75–1")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(score_bin)) %>%
  mutate(location = factor(location)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = location, fill = species_code)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  facet_wrap(~ score_bin) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  labs(
    x = "Location",
    y = "Proportion of detections",
    fill = "Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
Raw counts were validatd with results at @fig-audited.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| include: true
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-cap: Audited seabird detections
#| label: fig-audited

bcsi_he2 %>%
  mutate(
    score = as.numeric(species_individual_comments),
    score = ifelse(score < 0 | score > 1, NA, score),
    score_bin = cut(
      score,
      breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1),
      include.lowest = TRUE,
      labels = c("0–0.25", "0.25–0.5", "0.5–0.75", "0.75–1")
    ),
    score_bin = factor(
      score_bin,
      levels = c("0–0.25", "0.25–0.5", "0.5–0.75", "0.75–1")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(score_bin)) %>%
  mutate(location = factor(location)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = location, fill = species_code)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  facet_wrap(~ score_bin) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  labs(
    x = "Location",
    y = "Proportion of detections",
    fill = "Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Seabird relative abundance



## Discussion and recommendations
